language.select_description: Pick the language Steward should use for supported private replies, help, tutorials, and translated public message copies.
language.saved_description: Steward will use this language for supported guided messages.
language.coverage_body: Not every command supports translations yet. We are updating the bot gradually, starting with the member-facing commands and tutorials.
language.unsupported: That language is not supported yet.
translate.no_language: Choose a language first with `/language`, then press Translate again.
translate.not_available: A translated copy is not available for this message yet.
translate.copy_notice: Translated copy only. Use buttons, votes, signups, and reactions on the original message.
help.title: Steward Command Guide
help.description: Server-isolated GoTC coordination, roles, profiles, stats, and council tools.
help.no_detail: I do not have a detailed card for `/{command}` yet. Try `/help` for the full menu.
help.footer: Tip: use /help command:tutorial for a focused command card.
help.command.register.short: Create or update your GoTC profile.
help.command.add_alt.short: Add an alt under your account.
help.command.update_name.short: Rename your main or an alt.
help.command.update_access.short: Remove keep access from your list.
help.command.update_attack.short: Update primary troop attack and rally stats.
help.command.update_defense.short: Update primary troop defense and owned-SoP rein cap.
help.command.lookup_account.short: View owner, alts, and keep access.
help.command.bubble_up.short: Ping an owner and their access list.
help.command.time12.short: Post a 12-hour time as a Discord timestamp.
help.command.time24.short: Post a 24-hour time as a Discord timestamp.
help.detail.language.title: Choose your language
help.detail.language.details: Sets the language used for supported private replies, help, tutorials, and translated public message copies.
help.detail.tutorial.title: Guided walkthrough
help.detail.tutorial.details: Opens a private, button-driven walkthrough for registration, stats, alts, access, lookup, bubble alerts, and time commands.
help.detail.register.title: Create your profile
help.detail.register.details: Stores your profile inside this Discord server only. Timezone autocomplete keeps event times accurate.
help.detail.add_alt.title: Add an alt
help.detail.add_alt.details: Adds an alt under your profile and asks for its purpose using a dropdown.
help.detail.add_access.title: Grant keep access
help.detail.add_access.details: Adds someone to your keep access list so council can bubble the right people.
help.detail.update_access.title: Remove keep access
help.detail.update_access.details: Shows your access list privately and lets you remove someone from it.
help.detail.update_name.title: Rename main or alt
help.detail.update_name.details: Updates your main IGN or renames one of your registered alts.
help.detail.update_attack.title: Update attack stats
help.detail.update_attack.details: Opens a modal for primary troop stats only: marcher attack/defense/health vs player at seat of power, plus rally cap fields.
help.detail.update_defense.title: Update defense stats
help.detail.update_defense.details: Opens a modal for primary troop stats only: defense/attack/health vs player at seat of power, plus reinforcement cap at owned SoP.
help.detail.update_dragon_attack_stats.d: Opens a 4-field modal for dragon marcher attack, defense, health vs player at SoP, plus dragon attack vs dragon.
Opens a 3-field modal for dragon defense, attack, and health vs player at SoP.
help.detail.lookup_account.title: Look up an account
help.detail.lookup_account.details: Shows the account owner, tier, alts, and access list for the selected profile.
help.detail.bubble_up.title: Send a bubble alert
help.detail.bubble_up.details: Tags the account owner and everyone on their access list when a keep needs attention.
help.detail.time12.title: Post a 12-hour timestamp
help.detail.time12.details: Converts your registered timezone into a Discord timestamp everyone sees in their own local time.
help.detail.time24.title: Post a 24-hour timestamp
help.detail.time24.details: Converts your registered timezone into a Discord timestamp everyone sees in their own local time.
tutorial.done: Tutorial closed. Run `/tutorial` anytime you want the guide again.
tutorial.start.title: Start here: how Steward works
tutorial.start.description: This private guide walks you through the member commands in a safe order. Use the dropdown to jump, or press Next to go through everything.
tutorial.start.goal: Steward helps your server coordinate profiles, alts, combat stats, access lists, alerts, and event times.
tutorial.start.steps:
1. Choose your language with `/language`.
2. Register your main account with `/register`.
3. Add alts if you have them.
4. Update stats when your account changes.
5. Use access and bubble alerts when a keep needs attention.
tutorial.start.example: `/tutorial topic:stats` jumps straight to stats help.
tutorial.start.tips: Slash commands start with `/`. Discord will show boxes for the information Steward needs. Private tutorial replies are visible only to you.
tutorial.language.title: Choose your language first
tutorial.language.description: Do this before the tutorial if English is not your preferred language.
tutorial.language.goal: Your language choice controls supported private replies, help pages, tutorial pages, and Translate-button copies.
tutorial.language.steps:
1. Run `/language`.
2. Pick your language by its native name.
3. Run `/tutorial` again if you want this guide in that language.
tutorial.language.tips: Not every command is translated yet. Steward will show more translated messages as the language pack grows.
tutorial.register.title: Register your main account
tutorial.register.description: Registration tells Steward your GoTC name, troop tier, and timezone.
tutorial.register.goal: Use this once when joining a server, and again if your main profile details change.
tutorial.register.steps:
1. Run `/register`.
2. Enter your exact in-game name.
3. Pick your timezone from autocomplete, such as `Asia/Kolkata`.
4. Select your troop tier from the dropdown.
tutorial.register.tips: Your profile is saved only inside this Discord server. Your timezone helps `/time12`, `/time24`, and BG event planning.
tutorial.add_alt.title: Add an alt
tutorial.add_alt.description: Alts are extra accounts that belong under your main profile.
tutorial.add_alt.goal: Use this before updating stats for an alt. Steward only accepts stat updates for alts that already exist.
tutorial.add_alt.steps:
1. Run `/add_alt`.
2. Enter the alt's exact name.
3. Pick the alt purpose from the dropdown.
tutorial.add_alt.tips: Roster alts are separate from registered account alts. `/add_alt` is only for your personal profile and stats.
tutorial.stats.title: Update attack and defense stats
tutorial.stats.description: Stats commands open forms. Fill the fields and submit.
tutorial.stats.goal: Use these when your primary troop combat stats, rally cap, or reinforcement cap changes.
tutorial.stats.steps:
1. Use `/update_attack target:main` for your main account.
2. Use `/update_defense target:main` for defense stats.
3. For an alt, replace `main` with the exact alt name.
4. Submit the form Discord opens.
tutorial.stats.tips: User-facing text says defense, but the old Firestore field is still kept compatible behind the scenes.
tutorial.dragon.title: Update dragon stats
tutorial.dragon.description: Dragon stats are separate from regular attack and defense stats.
tutorial.dragon.goal: Use these when your dragon combat stats change.
tutorial.dragon.steps:
1. Run `/update_dragon_attack_stats target:main`.
2. Run `/update_dragon_defense_stats target:main`.
3. For alts, use the exact alt name as the target.
tutorial.dragon.tips: Dragon defense currently has three fields: defense, attack, and health vs player at Seat of Power.
tutorial.access.title: Give someone keep access
tutorial.access.description: Access lists tell council who can help with your keep.
tutorial.access.goal: Use this when another Discord member can shield, bubble, move, or manage your keep.
tutorial.access.steps:
1. Run `/add_access`.
2. Choose the Discord member.
3. Steward adds them to your access list.
tutorial.access.tips: Access is server-specific. Adding someone here does not affect other Discord servers.
tutorial.update_access.title: Remove keep access
tutorial.update_access.description: Use this when someone should no longer appear on your keep access list.
tutorial.update_access.goal: Keep your account safety list current.
tutorial.update_access.steps:
1. Run `/update_access`.
2. Steward privately shows your access list.
3. Pick the user to remove from the dropdown.
tutorial.update_access.tips: If the list is empty, Steward will tell you privately.
tutorial.lookup.title: Look up account details
tutorial.lookup.description: Lookup shows owner, tier, registered alts, and access list.
tutorial.lookup.goal: Use this when you need to know who owns an account or who has access.
tutorial.lookup.steps:
1. Run `/lookup_account`.
2. Choose a Discord member, or type an exact IGN.
3. Read the profile embed Steward returns.
tutorial.lookup.tips: Lookup searches only this server's registered profiles.
tutorial.bubble.title: Send a bubble alert
tutorial.bubble.description: Bubble alerts tag the owner and everyone on the account's access list.
tutorial.bubble.goal: Use this when a keep needs urgent attention.
tutorial.bubble.steps:
1. Run `/bubble_up`.
2. Choose a Discord member, or type the IGN.
3. Steward posts a public alert tagging the right people.
tutorial.bubble.tips: Use this only for real coordination needs so alerts stay meaningful.
tutorial.time.title: Share times that everyone understands
tutorial.time.description: Discord timestamps automatically show in each reader's local timezone.
tutorial.time.goal: Use these for rallies, events, reminders, and coordination plans.
tutorial.time.steps:
1. Make sure your timezone is saved with `/register`.
2. Use `/time12` for inputs like `8:30pm`.
3. Use `/time24` for inputs like `2030`.
4. Pick the date and optional message.
tutorial.time.example:
`/time12 time_input:8:30pm date_input:2026-07-09 extra_text:Rally at`
`/time24 hhmm:2030 date_input:2026-07-09 extra_text:Rally at`
tutorial.time.tips: The posted timestamp is public, but everyone sees it in their own local timezone.
tutorial.update_name.title: Rename your main or alt
tutorial.update_name.description: Use this when an IGN changes.
tutorial.update_name.goal: Keep lookup, stats, and access information easy to find.
tutorial.update_name.steps:
1. Run `/update_name`.
2. Enter the current name.
3. Enter the new name.
4. Steward updates the main profile or matching alt.
tutorial.update_name.tips: For alts, type the existing alt name exactly so Steward can find it.
tutorial.help.title: Help and next steps
tutorial.help.description: Use help when you know the command name but need a reminder.
tutorial.help.goal: Find examples quickly without asking another player.
tutorial.help.steps:
1. Run `/help` for the full menu.
2. Run `/help command:<command>` for a focused card.
3. Return to `/tutorial` when you want a guided walkthrough.
tutorial.help.tips: When a public message has a Translate button, choose `/language` first, then press Translate to get a private copy.
public.role_ids_footer: Role IDs are saved, so renamed roles keep working.
public.availability.title: Weekend Availability Check
public.availability.description: Open, Mid-Shift, and Close can be combined. Click again to remove. Throughout and Absent are exclusive.
public.role_panel.troop_tier.title: Troop Tier
public.role_panel.troop_tier.description: Choose the highest troop tier you want shown on your profile.
public.role_panel.primary_troop_type.des: Choose the troop type you usually lead with.
public.role_panel.specialist_roles.title: Specialist Roles
public.role_panel.specialist_roles.descr: Toggle any extra jobs you can cover.
public.role_panel.dragon_level.title: Dragon Level
public.role_panel.dragon_level.descripti: Choose the dragon threshold that best matches your account.
public.bg_ping_panel.title: Battleground Ping Opt-In
public.bg_ping_panel.description: Choose whether you want all battleground pings, Titans of the North pings, or The Great Ranging pings.
public.poll.footer_open: Use the Vote button on the original message to choose an option.
public.bg.title: Battleground: {type}
public.bg.footer: Use the buttons on the original message to sign up or leave.
tutorial.weekend.title: Weekend availability roles
tutorial.weekend.description: Weekend availability is a role panel. You click buttons to show when you can help.
tutorial.weekend.goal: Let leadership quickly see who is open, mid-shift, close, throughout, or absent for the weekend.
tutorial.weekend.steps:
1. Find the weekend availability panel posted by admins.
2. Click Open, Mid-Shift, Close, Throughout, or Absent.
3. Steward adds or removes the matching Discord role.
4. Open, Mid-Shift, and Close can stack. Throughout and Absent replace the other availability choices.
tutorial.weekend.example: If you can help at open and close, click both Open and Close.
tutorial.weekend.tips: Your click gives you a Discord role. Admins can use `/availability_report` to see everyone grouped by role.
tutorial.battlegrounds.title: Battleground signups and pings
tutorial.battlegrounds.description: Battleground posts let you sign up for a specific event and receive reminders.
tutorial.battlegrounds.goal: Opt into BG pings you care about and sign up only for the events you plan to attend.
tutorial.battlegrounds.steps:
1. Use the BG ping panel to opt into All BG, Titans, or Great Ranging pings.
2. When a BG event is posted, click Sign Up if you plan to attend.
3. Steward adds you to the event list.
4. Signed-up players are tagged 60, 30, 15, and 0 minutes before the event.
5. Click Leave if your plans change.
tutorial.battlegrounds.example: Clicking a BG ping role means you can be notified when events of that type are created. Clicking Sign Up means you get event reminders.
tutorial.battlegrounds.tips: Translate gives you a private copy of the BG post. Signups still happen only on the original message.

help.command.tutorial_admin.short: Open the guided admin setup tutorial.
help.detail.tutorial_admin.title: Admin setup walkthrough
help.detail.tutorial_admin.details: Opens a private admin guide for role setup, Logistics/Council setup, BG tools, polls, and roster management.
admin_tutorial.start.title: Admin tutorial: Steward setup order
admin_tutorial.start.description: This private guide explains what each admin tool is for and the cleanest order to set up a server.
admin_tutorial.start.goal: Give members a simple bot experience: clear roles, clear profile setup, clean event tools, and roster management that does not mix with account profiles.
admin_tutorial.start.steps:
1. Open this guide with `/tutorial_admin` whenever you need the setup flow again.
2. Confirm Steward has the Discord permissions it needs.
3. Run `/setup_gotc_roles`.
4. Set Logistics and Council roles.
5. Post member role panels and language/tutorial instructions.
6. Configure logs, autorole, BG pings, polls, and roster tools only where needed.
admin_tutorial.start.tips: Keep setup posts in a clear channel. Tell members to run `/language` first and `/tutorial` second.
admin_tutorial.permissions.title: Permissions and role position
admin_tutorial.permissions.description: Most setup issues are Discord permission or role hierarchy issues, not database issues.
admin_tutorial.permissions.goal: Make sure Steward can create/manage the roles and messages you expect it to manage.
admin_tutorial.permissions.steps:
1. Give the bot Manage Roles for role panels and autorole.
2. Move Steward's bot role above GoTC roles it should assign.
3. Give it Send Messages, Embed Links, Use Slash Commands, and Read Message History in coordination channels.
4. For Translate buttons, members press Translate on the original message and get a private translated copy.
admin_tutorial.permissions.example: If role buttons say Steward needs a higher role, move the bot role above the GoTC role group.
admin_tutorial.permissions.tips: Steward will not create autorole targets. Autorole uses only an existing role you choose.
admin_tutorial.roles.title: GoTC role setup and panels
admin_tutorial.roles.description: GoTC roles drive weekend availability, troop tier, troop type, specialist, dragon level, and battleground ping opt-ins.
admin_tutorial.roles.goal: Create or verify the role set once, save role IDs, then post clean panels for members.
admin_tutorial.roles.steps:
1. Run `/setup_gotc_roles`.
2. Run `/post_role_panel` for troop/type/specialist/dragon selection.
3. Run `/post_battleground_ping_panel` for BG opt-in roles.
4. Run `/availability_check` when you want a fresh weekend availability panel.
admin_tutorial.roles.example: Members click role panel controls themselves; admins do not need to manually assign every role.
admin_tutorial.roles.tips: Role IDs are saved in Firestore, so renamed Discord roles keep working after setup. Use `/availability_report` to see the current weekend availability groups.
admin_tutorial.logistics_council.title: Logistics and Council roles
admin_tutorial.logistics_council.descrip: Logistics is for trusted helpers. Council is for council announcements and poll closing.
admin_tutorial.logistics_council.goal: Give the right people operational access without giving them broad server moderation powers.
admin_tutorial.logistics_council.steps:
1. Run `/setup_logistics role:@Role`.
2. Run `/setup_council role:@Role`.
3. Run `/setup_announcements channel:#channel` if council announcements should go to a specific channel.
4. Use `/announce` for council announcements after the channel is configured.
5. Tell Logistics they can use roster management and admin-proxy profile/stat commands.
admin_tutorial.logistics_council.tips: Admin always passes Logistics checks. Normal members get the shared denial message.
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.title: Logs and autorole
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.description: Server logs and autorole are optional server-management helpers.
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.goal: Keep join/leave logs in one configured channel and assign an existing starter role safely.
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.steps:
1. Run `/set_logs_channel channel:#logs` if you want join/leave logs.
2. Run `/set_autorole role:@Member enabled:True` if new members should get an existing role.
3. Use `/disable_logs_channel` to turn logs off.
4. Use `/disable_autorole` to stop automatic role assignment.
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.example: Use `/server_settings` to see current logs and autorole configuration.
admin_tutorial.logs_autorole.tips: The autorole must be below Steward's top role and cannot be managed by another bot/integration.
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.title: Member onboarding flow
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.descrip: The simplest member path is language, tutorial, registration, roles, stats, access.
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.goal: Reduce support questions by giving members a predictable first-run order.
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.steps:
1. Ask everyone to run `/language`.
2. Ask everyone to run `/tutorial`.
3. Ask them to run `/register`.
4. Have them choose roles from posted role panels.
5. Ask hitters/reins to update stats and add access users.
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.example: Pinned message idea: `Start with /language, then /tutorial, then /register.`
admin_tutorial.member_onboarding.tips: The tutorial is private, so it will not clutter your channels.
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.title: Battleground event flow
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.description: BG events create signup posts and reminder messages for signed-up players.
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.goal: Let players opt into BG pings and then sign up for specific BG events.
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.steps:
1. Run `/setup_gotc_roles` so BG ping roles exist.
2. Run `/post_battleground_ping_panel`.
3. Members opt into All BG, Titans, or Great Ranging pings.
4. Create events with `/create_battlegrounds_event`.
5. Signed-up players are reminded 60, 30, 15, and 0 minutes before start.
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.example: If a member clicks Sign Up on a BG event, they are added to that event's reminder list.
admin_tutorial.battlegrounds.tips: The event uses the creator's registered timezone. Ask event creators to register before making BG events.
admin_tutorial.polls.title: Council polls
admin_tutorial.polls.description: Polls are Firestore-backed so votes survive restarts.
admin_tutorial.polls.goal: Run lightweight council votes without losing results when Render restarts.
admin_tutorial.polls.steps:
1. Use `/poll question:<text> options:A, B, C`.
2. Members click Vote and choose an option privately.
3. Results update on the public poll message.
4. Council role members can close the poll.
admin_tutorial.polls.example: User-written poll questions and options are not translated. Bot labels around them can be translated.
admin_tutorial.polls.tips: Use clear, short option names. Discord supports up to 25 options.
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.title: Roster management overview
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.descripti: The Legion roster is a lightweight seat-placement board, not a member-account database.
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.goal: Manage T1-T4 seat placement without connecting roster entries to Discord owners, stats, access lists, or `/add_alt`.
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.steps:
Roster entries have only six fields: `serial`, `name`, `seat_tier`, `parent_serial`, `type`, `expiry`.
Capacity is T1: 1, T2: 5, T3: 25, T4: 125, total 156.
`type` is blank, `PA`, or `TA`.
`expiry` matters only for temporary alts (`TA`).
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.example: A T4 entry points to a T3 parent by `parent_serial`. A T3 points to T2. A T2 points to T1.
admin_tutorial.roster_overview.tips: Do not use roster for ownership, troop tier, locks, slot indexes, main-account flags, or combat stats.
admin_tutorial.roster_import.title: Roster import
admin_tutorial.roster_import.description: Import is for a full roster CSV, with validation before anything is applied.
admin_tutorial.roster_import.goal: Safely create or replace the current roster after checking hierarchy and capacity.
admin_tutorial.roster_import.steps:
1. Run `/roster_template` and fill the CSV.
2. Run `/roster_import file:<csv>`.
3. Review errors/warnings and counts.
4. If clean, click Apply Draft.
5. Run `/roster_validate` later when you want to check the active roster again.
6. If replacing an existing roster, use `replace_existing:True` intentionally.
admin_tutorial.roster_import.tips: Missing TA expiry dates are defaulted using roster config. Validation errors block applying the draft.
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.title: Weekly roster updates
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.descriptio: Weekly updates create a preview draft from requested T1-T4 placements before applying changes.
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.goal: Move requested names, handle overflow, protect key names, and preview all changes before saving.
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.steps:
1. Use `/roster_update_positions` with a CSV or pasted text.
2. Text supports sections like `T1: Name | T2: A, B | PROTECT: Name | REMOVE:`.
3. `mode:ask` shows conflicts instead of auto-moving people.
4. `mode:auto` accepts automatic moves.
5. Apply only after reviewing forced changes, cascade changes, benching, conflicts, and validation.
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.example: `/roster_update_positions text:T1: vix | T2: Gov, Alex | PROTECT: vix mode:ask`
admin_tutorial.roster_updates.tips: Use `PROTECT` for names you strongly prefer not to demote. Use `REMOVE` for names to bench/remove.
admin_tutorial.roster_expiry_exports.des: Temporary alts can expire, and the roster can be exported without touching registered account data.
admin_tutorial.roster_expiry_exports.goa: Keep TAs current and export the seat board when leadership needs a copy.
admin_tutorial.roster_expiry_exports.ste:
1. Use `/roster_config` to set default TA days, warning days, and alert channel.
2. Use `/roster_alts filter:expired` or `filter:expiring`.
3. Use `/roster_update_expiry` for all, new, or specific TAs.
4. Use `/roster_export format:csv/json/text` for roster-only exports.
admin_tutorial.roster_expiry_exports.tip: Roster exports are separate from `/account_export`; do not merge roster seats with registered profiles unless you intentionally build a migration later.
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.title: Common admin issues
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.descripti: Most problems come from missing setup, missing role hierarchy, or using the wrong roster/account command.
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.goal: Fix confusion quickly without changing data paths.
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.steps:
If role buttons fail, check bot role height.
If Translate says no language, ask the member to run `/language`.
If roster import fails, read validation errors before changing CSV.
If stats target fails, confirm the alt was added with `/add_alt`.
Use `/get_stats` to verify saved combat stats.
Use `/moderate` only for Discord kicks/bans, not GoTC profile cleanup.
If slash commands do not appear, redeploy/resync Discord commands.
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.example: Roster command for seat board: `/roster_export`. Registered account export: `/account_export`.
admin_tutorial.troubleshooting.tips: Keep Firestore server-isolated under `guilds/{guild_id}`. Do not create global user paths casually.
Create a BG signup with reminders.
help.command.setup_gotc_roles.short: Create or verify all GoTC roles.
help.command.post_role_panel.short: Post troop, type, specialist, and dragon panels.
help.command.set_autorole.short: Set an existing role for new members.
help.command.add_register.short: Register a member on their behalf.
help.command.add_update.short: Update a member's main or alt stats.
help.command.roster_template.short: Download the full roster CSV template.
help.command.roster_import.short: Preview and import a full seat roster.
help.command.roster_validate.short: Check hierarchy, capacity, and TA expiry.
help.command.roster_export.short: Export roster as CSV, JSON, or text.
help.command.roster_config.short: Configure TA expiry defaults and alerts.
help.detail.get_stats.title: View combat stats
help.detail.get_stats.details: Shows saved offense, defense, dragon offense, and dragon defense stats for a registered account.
help.detail.add_register.title: Admin register
help.detail.add_register.details: Admin or Logistics shortcut that writes the selected member's profile to the same Firestore path as self-registration.
help.detail.add_update.title: Admin stat update
help.detail.add_update.details: Admin or Logistics stat modal for updating a selected member's main account or an already registered alt. Use `attack` or `defense`.
help.detail.setup_logistics.title: Set Logistics role
help.detail.setup_logistics.details: Admin-only setup. The Logistics role can use admin-proxy registration/stat commands and Legion roster management commands, but not moderation or server settings.
help.detail.poll.title: Create a poll
help.detail.poll.details: Creates a live poll whose votes are stored in Firestore, so Render restarts do not wipe results.
help.detail.availability_check.title: Start availability check
help.detail.availability_check.details: Clears old weekend availability roles and posts a fresh panel. Open, Mid-Shift, and Close can stack; Throughout and Absent are exclusive.
help.detail.availability_report.title: Show availability report
help.detail.availability_report.details: Shows members grouped by the current weekend availability roles.
help.detail.setup_gotc_roles.title: Create GoTC roles
help.detail.setup_gotc_roles.details: Creates or verifies Steward's GoTC roles, saves their role IDs, and keeps renamed roles working later.
help.detail.post_role_panel.title: Post role panels
help.detail.post_role_panel.details: Posts persistent role selection panels for troop tier, primary type, specialist roles, and dragon level, including L55+ Rally Keep.
help.detail.post_battleground_ping_panel: Posts buttons for all battleground pings, Titans of the North, and The Great Ranging.
help.detail.create_battlegrounds_event.d: Uses your registered timezone, posts a signup list, and reminds signed-up players 60, 30, 15, and 0 minutes before start.
help.detail.set_logs_channel.title: Set logs channel
help.detail.set_logs_channel.details: Sends join and leave logs only to the configured channel.
help.detail.disable_logs_channel.title: Disable logs
help.detail.disable_logs_channel.details: Turns off Steward join and leave logs.
help.detail.set_autorole.title: Set autorole
help.detail.set_autorole.details: Uses an existing assignable role for new members. Steward will not create roles.
help.detail.disable_autorole.title: Disable autorole
help.detail.disable_autorole.details: Stops automatic role assignment for new members.
help.detail.server_settings.title: Server settings
help.detail.server_settings.details: Shows the configured log channel and autorole state.
help.detail.moderate.title: Moderate a member
help.detail.moderate.details: Kick or ban a member. This uses Discord moderation permissions and is separate from Steward logistics access.
help.detail.setup_council.title: Set Council role
help.detail.setup_council.details: Admin-only setup. The Council role can close polls and use council-only announcement tools.
help.detail.setup_announcements.title: Set announcement channel
help.detail.setup_announcements.details: Admin-only setup. Council announcements are sent to this configured channel.
help.detail.announce.title: Send council announcement
help.detail.announce.details: Council-only command that posts an @everyone announcement embed in the configured announcement channel.
help.detail.account_export.title: Export account data
help.detail.account_export.details: Exports registered member profiles, alts, and saved combat stats. Legion seat rosters use `/roster_export` instead.
help.detail.roster_template.title: Download roster template
help.detail.roster_template.details: Returns the exact CSV structure for the lightweight Legion seat roster.
help.detail.roster_import.title: Import roster
help.detail.roster_import.details: Admin or Logistics. Validates a full roster CSV, fixes missing TA expiry defaults, then shows an Apply/Cancel preview.
help.detail.roster_validate.title: Validate roster
help.detail.roster_validate.details: Checks the active Legion roster for hierarchy, capacity, and temporary-alt expiry warnings.
help.detail.roster_export.title: Export roster
help.detail.roster_export.details: Exports the lightweight Legion seat roster as CSV, JSON, or text. Registered account data uses `/account_export`.
help.detail.roster_update_positions.usag:
`/roster_update_positions file:<csv> mode:auto`
`/roster_update_positions text:T1: vix | T2: t2alt, IamT31, Gov | PROTECT: vix, Gov mode:ask`
help.detail.roster_update_positions.deta: Admin or Logistics. Text supports multiline sections or one-line pipe sections. Sections: T1, T2, T3, T4, PROTECT, REMOVE, MODE. Names can be comma-separated. Empty sections are allowed.
help.detail.roster_alts.title: Roster alts
help.detail.roster_alts.details: Lists only roster PA/TA entries. This is separate from the registered /add_alt system.
help.detail.roster_config.title: Roster config
help.detail.roster_config.details: Admin or Logistics. Configures default TA expiry, warning window, and daily expiry alert channel.
help.detail.roster_update_expiry.title: Update TA expiry
help.detail.roster_update_expiry.details: Admin or Logistics. Batch updates expiry dates for temporary alts by all TAs, newly added TAs, or specific names.
